chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT LARKANA C.P.No.D-598 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objections at flag "A". 2. For orders on M.A.No.2022/2025 (E/A). 3. For hearing of main case. ------ 21.08.2025 Syed Kazim Raza Shah, Advocate for the Petitioner. Mr. Oshaq Ali Sangi, Assistant Attorney General for Pakistan. ------ Thorough this petition, the petitioner has prayed for following relief: (a). That this Honourable Court may be pleased to direct the respondents to decide the departmental appeals of the petitioner as mentioned herein above forthwith. Learned counsel submits that the petitioner has filed Departmental Appeal(s), available on the Court file as Annexures “A” to “E”, pages 13 to 21, which are pending adjudication since 2017; however, the respondents have not taken any decision or decided the same. He, therefore, submits that the petitioner will be satisfied and would not press this petition, if this Court, by disposing of instant petition, issue directions to the respondents to decide the fate of the Departmental Appeal(s) filed by the petitioner before the department within shortest possible time. Mr. Oshaq Ali Sangi, Assistant Attorney General for Pakistan, present in the Court in connection with some other matters, waives notice of instant petition and after going through the prayer clause does not oppose the proposal so advanced by the counsel for the petitioner. Since a simple prayer has been made as Departmental Appeals, as is evident from the file, are pending before the respondents since 2017, therefore, respondents are directed to hear the petitioner after due notice to all the concerned as well provide equal opportunity of hearing to them and decide it preferably within a period of three months under intimation to this Court through learned Additional Registrar.

Oshaq Ali Sangi, Assistant Attorney General for Pakistan, present in the Court in connection with some other matters, waives notice of instant petition and after going through the prayer clause does not oppose the proposal so advanced by the counsel for the petitioner. Since a simple prayer has been made as Departmental Appeals, as is evident from the file, are pending before the respondents since 2017, therefore, respondents are directed to hear the petitioner after due notice to all the concerned as well provide equal opportunity of hearing to them and decide it preferably within a period of three months under intimation to this Court through learned Additional Registrar. A copy of this order alongwith copies of Departmental Appeals available as annexures “A” to “E”, pages 13 to 21 shall be communicated to the respondents for compliance through all modes of service including fax as well as Courier except publication. The charges of the courier shall be borne by the petitioner. Instant petition stands disposed of in the above terms. Judge Judge Manzoor
